item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't love it if it's just intended to be a natural thing. That all seals would hate him and use their normal ability to talk to express that hatred. I do like it if it's assumed to be a human-induced thing. Like it's in some way a human idea that has spread to the seals that's making them take on this hatred. Like maybe a magical or type-green person saw him on the beach, and it's both that person's anomaly along with the nature of the act that created the current situation, not just the act itself. Kinda like with the skip that has spiders behaving like communists, but it's unclear if it's just the spiders behaving normally and being impacted by the information, or if it has something more to do with the dead guy who introduced them to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: me? i didn't do this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what if he changes his to Karl? or something else entirely?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Carl you rascal, +1

Hey Carl!
Did it hurt when you fell out of someone's ass and into the toilet, you piece of shit?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably chubbed a baby seal if you know what I mean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fucking hilarious +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not.

"lolFoundation" means its the research staff who are acting like fratboys and getting into Wacky Hijinkstm.

Something isn't lolFoundation just because the SCP itself is absurd or humorous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's entertaining, but, it's me, or this is the much criticized "LOLFoundation".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find it funny, but being a -J SCP would ultimately suit this better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll give you a hint, in the form of a horrible joke.

A baby seal walks into a club.

item.title: Good SCP!
item.content:encoded: VERY FUNNY!! I always thought of seals as cute and cuddly, until they start cursing you and giving you death threats XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t know what upset pinnipeds have to do with dark and stormy nights, other than the author’s introductory post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is delightfully bizarre! I'd suggest moving the examples of pinnipeds into a footnote, but that's just a personal preference. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …. Did he… Did he club a baby seal? O_o I usually just lurk but this one made me comment… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is actually Series IV.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't like the silly/cheekiness to this. It seems a lot of series 3 SCPs are just becoming huge jokes. -1

item.title: Community Outreach Team Captain Post
item.content:encoded: As the author has been disqualified for grossly poor sportsmanship, this entry has been retroactively removed from the Jam Contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is so freaking funny and surreal. a man who is being screamed insults at by seals… you really must have quite an imagination lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Its not amazing, but its pretty good. I like that bit at the end and it genuinely does make me wonder what he did to piss off every seal in the world. Its probably not fair of me to give this a vote before I can actually watch the video (I'm at work), but I thought, on its own it's funny and some what mysterious. I wouldn't put it on a top 100 list of SCPs, but I would give it an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Carl, What was your fault?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad. pretty creative.

CAAAAAAAAARRRRRRLLLLLLL WHAT DID YOU DO?!?!?!?!?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this! The fact that some random dude, doing something naughty or just plain immoral with pinnipeds, ends up getting cursed with all pinnipeds wishing to return the favor is plain hilarious! The article has a nice flow, ending with an interesting mystery.

The only negative things I have to say are:

  • This is a nitpick, but the video was unnecessary. Everything it conveyed could've been done in text.
  • Given the intended tone of regular SCP articles, I would say this should be a joke SCP. The premise is funny enough, but as a joke? Rick and Morty, Looney Tunes, etc - driving things over-the-top would make it fantastic!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I tend to like unusual Scips that do something… different. Doesn't matter what it is, really, just something that stounds out a bit from "thing what is anomalous".

I don't think there's any issue with the tone; it's clearly not lolFoundation, and I don't see why every mainlister has to be a super serious grimdark-trip. This is fine to me.

+1

item.title: Upvote
item.content:encoded:
I find this sooo funny!

+1 to you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fucking Carl. Who the fuck does he think he is?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We’ll soon need a tag update. After all, pinnipeds are cute. Go to hell, Carl.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An original idea, did you had a nightmare with seals and then write this?, because this is one of the SCPs that I cannot imagine in what did the creators based

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1

I appreciate the work that went into this skip and the video. But to me, there's not really enough substance here. There's no real emotional connection to me, it's not particularly unique or creepy, and there's no mystery to solve [more below].

+ show block
– hide block
  • I do not believe the video enhances the article whatsoever. I do not think it weakens it, either.
  • The dialogue is fine. Maybe a bit redundant.
  • As stated above, there's just not enough here:
    • I'm not emotionally connected enough to Carl to care about his unusual plight
    • I'm not emotionally connected to the seals and what Carl -may- have done to them
    • His situation, while unusual, isn't quite unique or quirky enough to stand out on its own.
    • There's no real mystery to solve. Yeah, you leave us with an open question, but there's no possible way for us, as the readers, to put together any pieces from the article to figure it out. And either way, I'm not sure the resolution enhances the article. Maybe Carl clubbed and ate a baby seal one night. Maybe he lives in Nebraska and never visited the coast. In either circumstance, I'm not sure the article is improved.

Having said all of this, I have no articles on the site with this high of a reception. So I congratulate you on your success and I hope your next article is something that I can get behind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the DM I really appreciate it. So first the general idea is really funny and it accomplishes humour and the image adds to that. I think a guy who has a strange effect on seals is an interesting idea especially since seals are a target for humans. Now my nitpicks are: I don't think euclid is necessary since when he isn't exposed to seals he's non anomalous and we understand his properties to a degree.I'm kinda disappointed that we don't have much else other than its anomalous effect on seals it'd be cool if it made seals hostile to all humans and made them more intelligent and dangerous since it would make the euclid class more appropriate. I'd also think this would be better if we knew what he did and the article explored the doubts of the sins he commited. +1 just for how funny this is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [Okay, so apparently the longer one did post as well. Apologies.]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Temporarily back from the dead to throw in my two cents. It's alright. The setup is concise and well-written, and as others have said, the ending is just the right amount of ambiguity to make the anomaly itself compelling. That said…

+ show block
– hide block

The video was cute, but very immersion-breaking. I'm surprised to see no one else mention it. Yes, we all know that ultimately it's (presumably) you just speaking over some stock footage of seals, but the point is to make us think the exact opposite. You're not (to my knowledge) a special effects wiz who could whip up a CGI seal, but this is a main series article and not a -J, and ultimately the end result does not help the article. I think it could be safely removed with little detriment. The paragraph after it is kind of jarring. Why would those seals lure the dude into a false sense of security when the others just start cursing him out immediately? It's forced and weird and doesn't really work well.

Subject vocalized "Fuck off, Carl!" prior to SCP-3213 being removed from the area.

I don't really see the necessity of this beyond 'hoy hoy goofiness'. The premise itself is silly enough, you don't need to oversaturate it.

Seal 2: Carl, how's it feel to know that even though you're going bald, you'll never get any uglier?

Sick roast. In all seriousness, this is comparatively way too lame and childish an insult. The seal dialogue is pretty okay throughout, but this felt out of place.

SCP-3213: Why?

Odd thing for the dude to ask. It's kind of weird that he'd enable the seals at all, frankly, considering we're made to believe he's been harassed by pinnipeds his whole life. But, you know, gotta slip that shock stinger ending line in somewhere.

All in all, it's a pretty cut-and-dry goofy mainlist article that has nothing horrible or exceptional about it. I don't see mediocrity as a bad thing, but in my eyes it certainly falls into that category.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1. As much as I love works with high stakes and heavy gravitas, I wish the site had more silly skips like this in the mainlist.

Honestly, it almost feels like a -J. The 'mystery' keeps it like a real skip. Don't add why seals hate Carl or it'll really feel like a -J.

Agreed. I waffled over whether it works better on the mainlist or as a -J, but I ended up on the mainlist side. I think the ambiguity makes it seem less like a joke and more like a silly effect that the Foundation is treating with their usual seriousness. I should add that this is heavily affected by my headcanon for this particular skip, which is that Carl didn't actually do anything; his anomalous effect is simply that when in the presence of seals and walruses, they develop an irrational hatred of him and believe he did something and want to yell at him for it. With that in mind, it's less "two irrational sides argue with each other for stupid reasons" and more "what compels these animals to verbally destroy this poor guy", which makes it feel more like a mainlist article.

Well I'm glad I was directed to this
Poor mate

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Its a good idea but this seems like it belongs more with the FIB UIU more due to its nature and easy containment but only if Carl was in the USA if he was not in the USA then I could see the foundation coming in to do some research on him but I'm not sure.
Just a minor change that I would add is the last name of Carl is redacted or blacked out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1.

This, like 2337, is just one of those articles so weird, so silly, so stupid genius, that it has to exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fuck off Cimm.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like it. It has a comedic, surreal aspect that effectively just barely covers the skip's dark underbelly. Not knowing what carl did makes me much more disturbed than whatever it could be. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subject vocalized "Fuck off, Carl!" prior to SCP-3213 being removed from the area.

A'ight this has my attention now.

Under no circumstances is SCP-3213 to be allowed unsupervised interaction with any member of any pinniped species

When SCP-3213 is in the direct line of sight of any member of any pinniped species, the animal will become sapient and capable of vocalization.

I'm sold for this idea.

That video is hilarious. +1
Honestly, it almost feels like a -J. The 'mystery' keeps it like a real skip. Don't add why seals hate Carl or it'll really feel like a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
cause then we couldn't hurt you any more, Carl.

Big fucking mood. +1

item.title: MOD POST - INFO - OPEN
item.content:encoded:
WNat13 this kind of comment should have been handled with a PM, not a "call out post" on the article. It reflects very poorly on you.

Please don't post things like this in the future. And you are correct, downvoting like that would be considered malicious downvoting.

item.title: ***EDITED***
item.content:encoded: *Removed offending comment. My apologies to author and mods, and I will handle issues through proper channels in the future.*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The picture's caption made me double-take to see if this was a -J, and gave me a sense of unease in a roundabout way by setting my expectations off-kilter as things progressed in earnest.

The seals' dialogue felt like the weakest part of this, excluding the final test; everything prior felt more humdrum than impactful. With test 5 in particular, unless I'm missing some intended implications, it feels like the information gleaned therein is covered well enough through the description.

On the other hand, the presentation of this as an unbidden phenomenon right up until the end strengthened the reveal nicely. I'm not sure if there's a way to work in some subtle lead-up through the dialogue without spoiling that effect. The ending is definitely what swung me away from a neutral feeling, and left the mind buzzing with curiosity as it wrapped up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, it seems you've gone with the "Incident happens in past, all aquatic pinniped animals hate Carl for life" theme. Nothing wrong with that. However I do think you could have changed up the behavior of the pinniped's actions towards Carl. Not make them act like a human. I can understand the aggressive tendency of the seals against Carl, but…why exactly do they just vocalize "Fuck off, Carl"? Why don't they try to be more aggressive than try to be a teenage human and start shit? That being said, I did give this a plus one due to the fact that you left an "Imagine what happened" moment at the end of the last interview. So all and all, it's sort of a neutral side for me. Good work though. Just needs a bit more improvement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, there's something about that. Either Carl did something serious and the seals are harassing him with good reason, OR the seals are bashing him for little to no reason, like some sort of seal witch hunt. A twitter mob implanted onto all seals that see the man.

Feels like it needs to push one direction over the other, imho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What dark secrets are you hiding from us Carl? What did you do?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is dumb and random .. i love it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot to be said about being able to make something short and sweet in a short amount of time, and that's what I get out of this article. I find the animals aggressive behavior towards Carl funny in a charming way, and the interview logs interesting. I'd like to have some more interview logs or one without the animals but just Carl, but not having those logs doesn't take away from the article. Great job overall, so +1 here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, the idea of a bunch of seals just being really pissed off at one guy named Carl, and even trying to harm him in any way possible (which I visualized as them just jumping out of the water and smacking Carl with their bodies) is goddamn hilarious, but it feels like the article tries to switch gears at the end from "stupidly silly" to "somewhat serious", and it didn't really work. I think either fully going for "Seals know something that Carl did" or "Seals just unreasonably hate Carl and hurl insults at him" woulda made the article a lot better. (Personally would have gone with the latter)

Might be just me though. And by the looks of this discussion thread, it's probably just me.

Nonvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: CAARRLLLLLL THAT KILLS PEOPLE!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea behind this, but as said in comments above, I think it would be better off as a -J. But, this is a borderline case, and seeing humorous scips on the mainlist is something I like.

I feel like there could also be a deeper story behjnd this that people could headcanon. Just think— Carl, what the fuck did you do?

+1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so stupid.

I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll upvote, because I like how leaves to the imagination what Carl has done to someone or to the seals (not necessarily killing them)…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A great quickly presented idea that leaves just enough to the imagination. When we talk about how you don't need a full plot and in-depth story to create a meaningful SCP, this is a great example. There's still enough detail that we know there's more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoa whoa whoa! This guy gets bullied and attacked by every single seal that sees him; the Foundation contains him and lets seals maul him to within an inch of his life for testing purposes; and then everyone in the discussion page here just isntantly takes the seals' side?

Poor Carl… this is ssooo sad!

Okay, joking aside (well, maybe half-joking…) I did like the article, so +1. If you've never seen a seal in the water, those things can move! They would kick a dude's ass for sure, that's gotta be legit scary for Carl.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddammit, Carl.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 cuz fuck Carl. He knows what he did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like I, personally, think it's silly to do that if you liked the article itself. That said there are least 2 downvotes on this article for that reason so I don't think you're alone in your dislike of the thing. That said it's a stylistic choice I stand by (and one I've been employing since like my very first tale several years ago).

And I appreciate that you just no-voted instead of the other thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is kind of petty, but I'm going to have to no-vote because of the obnoxiously long title you gave this in the mainlist. Hopefully that isn't against the rules since it has to do with the SCP itself - I'm getting really sick of needlessly long titles and this was the straw that broke the camel's back.
